name: Greedy
description: Questions on greedy algorithms and when they are optimal.

questions.question: A greedy algorithm makes:
choices: The globally optimal choice at each step
A locally optimal choice at each step
questions.explanation: Greedy picks a locally optimal choice; it is globally optimal only for certain problems.

questions.question: Which problem is often solved greedily?
questions.explanation: Activity selection (pick max non-overlapping intervals) is classic for 'earliest end time' greedy.

questions.question: Huffman coding uses a greedy strategy based on:
questions.explanation: Huffman repeatedly combines the two smallest frequency nodes, a greedy choice.

questions.question: Greedy does NOT always give the correct answer for:
questions.explanation: 0/1 knapsack requires DP or backtracking; greedy by value/weight can fail.

questions.question: To prove a greedy algorithm is correct, you often show:
choices: It runs in O(n log n)
A greedy choice is in some optimal solution
questions.explanation: Proof usually shows the greedy choice is safe (contained in some optimal solution) and optimal substructure holds.
